must_have_breakdown.skill: Salesforce Platform Management or Salesforce Architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume shows DevOps/release management focus rather than platform architecture ownership; managed environments and CI/CD but no explicit architecture design responsibilities mentioned.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Expert-level proficiency with Copado for CI/CD and release orchestration
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Copado listed as primary DevOps tool, used on Apple and Advance Auto Parts projects, holds Copado Fundamentals I & II, AI Certified, and was selected as a Copado Mentor.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Deep understanding of Salesforce multi-tenant architecture
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume references multi-org deployments and sandbox management but does not explicitly demonstrate deep multi-tenant architecture knowledge or governor limits expertise.
No explicit mention of governor limits; SOQL is listed in tools but no evidence of hands-on Apex/trigger tuning within governor limit constraints.
Resume states 'configuring and administering Salesforce sandboxes, ensuring optimal functionality' and 'environment strategy' across multiple projects.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Communication skills to translate technical releases into business-friendly roadmaps
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume mentions collaborating with cross-functional teams and cutover planning but no explicit evidence of producing business-friendly roadmaps or stakeholder communication artifacts.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Experience managing Business Critical production environments
must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume references production releases, hotfix management, and 'performance excellence' on PMI project but does not explicitly mention Business Critical tier or Salesforce premier support.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Working with Salesforce premier support tiers

must_have_breakdown.evidence: CGI role covers Salesforce Admin and development; certifications include Platform Developer I and App Builder confirming development environment familiarity.

must_have_breakdown.skill: Custom objects, workflows, triggers, SOQL & SOSL
must_have_breakdown.evidence: SOQL is listed in tools and Admin/Dev certifications imply custom objects and workflows, but triggers, SOSL, and Apex coding are not explicitly demonstrated in project work.
Resume mentions 'scalable and efficient DevOps practices' and CI/CD automation but no explicit reference to Force.com design patterns or architectural best practices.
Leads DevOps processes as a Technology Lead but resume does not explicitly state independent work or minimal supervision context.

reasoning: Vaishnavi is a strong Salesforce DevOps and Release Management specialist with 7 years of experience and excellent Copado credentials, including the rare distinction of being a Copado Mentor. Her core CI/CD, environment management, and multi-org deployment skills align well with major aspects of this role. However, the JD demands a platform manager who also architects federated development strategies, establishes Centers of Excellence, owns platform governance, and has deep Apex/LWC development chops alongside release management. Vaishnavi's profile skews heavily toward release engineering and DevOps tooling rather than platform architecture or hands-on Salesforce development depth. Key gaps include no evidence of Salesforce premier support tier experience, limited Apex/trigger/LWC development evidence, no explicit multi-tenant architecture or governor limits depth, and no demonstrable experience translating technical roadmaps for business audiences. She is a strong practitioner in her lane but does not yet demonstrate the breadth of a Senior Platform Manager who must also architect and govern the entire Salesforce ecosystem.
strengths: Expert Copado proficiency with multiple certifications (Fundamentals I & II, AI Certified) and selected as a Copado Mentor — a rare, peer-recognised credential that signals top-tier expertise in the JD's primary CI/CD tool.
Hands-on multi-org CI/CD pipeline management across high-profile clients (Apple, Philip Morris International, Advance Auto Parts), demonstrating real-world release orchestration at enterprise scale.

Broad Salesforce certification portfolio including Administrator, Platform Developer I, App Builder, Business Analyst, Agentforce Specialist, and AI Associate, covering both admin and developer tracks relevant to this role.
Flosum certification at both Professional and Expert levels adds a rare secondary DevOps tooling credential that few Copado specialists possess, expanding her deployment toolchain versatility.
Direct Agentforce Specialist certification and Agentforce listed as a key skill directly addresses the JD requirement to support Agentforce and Data Cloud release environments.
gaps: No evidence of working with Salesforce premier support tiers or managing 'Business Critical' designated production environments — a specific must-have that signals escalation management maturity the JD explicitly requires.

Limited Apex, Visualforce, LWC, or Aura development experience evidenced in the resume; the JD requires hands-on custom objects, triggers, SOSL, and design patterns to architect and govern the platform, not just deploy it.

No demonstrated experience establishing a Center of Excellence or architecting a federated development strategy — core strategic responsibilities of this Senior Platform Manager role that go beyond release engineering.
No quantified outcomes anywhere in the resume; no metrics on deployment frequency improvements, defect reduction rates, or release cycle times make it difficult to assess actual impact delivered.

Salesforce Data Cloud hands-on experience (ingestion, modeling, activation) is listed as a nice-to-have and mentioned in the JD scope but absent from the resume — relevant given the JD explicitly covers Data Cloud release management.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume and JD does not specify a maximum notice period, so no penalty applies.
JD work structure is not specified as In-office, so location mismatch penalty does not apply.
Candidate has 7 years of experience, well above the minimum threshold of 2.5 years (half of the 5-year JD requirement).
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
JD has no college preference or exclusion specified, so no adjustment applies.
